metadata.purpose: Ensure consistent translation of technical terms across languages.

terms.Aire: The name of the application (do not translate).
terms.AI: Artificial Intelligence (may be kept as AI).
terms.API key: Secret token used to authenticate with an AI provider.
terms.provider: AI service such as OpenAI, Anthropic, Google AI, Ollama, etc.
terms.model: Specific AI model like GPT-4o, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, etc.
terms.tool: An action the AI can request Aire to perform (e.g., read file, browse web).
terms.MCP: Model Context Protocol - a protocol for connecting external services.
terms.Ollama: Local AI server for running models on your own machine.
terms.Claude.ai: Anthropic's web interface for Claude models.
terms.Local API: Aire's internal API allowing other local apps to control it.
terms.voice output: Text-to-speech feature that reads AI responses aloud.
terms.voice input: Speech recognition that converts spoken words to text.
terms.system tray: Area near the clock where Aire runs in the background.
terms.auto‑accept: Profile that automatically approves certain AI actions.
terms.tool categories: Groups of tools like Files, Browser, Mouse, etc.
terms.capability tests: Checks to see what a provider/model can do.
terms.custom model: User-defined JSON configuration for a model's capabilities.
terms.native function calling: Structured tool calling via provider API.
terms.text‑based tool calling: Tool calls embedded in text using XML/JSON blocks.
terms.placeholder: Variable like {0} that will be replaced with a value at runtime.
terms.token: A piece of data used for authentication.
terms.quota: Usage limit imposed by an AI provider.
terms.rate limit: Restriction on how many requests can be made in a period.
terms.self‑hosted: Running software on your own server.
terms.health check: A test to verify a service is working.
